title: Business Analyst
description:
Job Title: Business Analyst (BPMN / DMN)

Company: Blue Polaris

Location: Pune, India (Hybrid)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience Level: 5 - 7Years




About Blue Polaris

Blue Polaris is a forward-thinking technology company focused on delivering cutting-edge automation and business solutions to global clients. We are committed to innovation, collaboration, and empowering our team to build efficient, intelligent systems that drive impactful business outcomes.

We’re currently looking for a Mid-level IBM BAW Developer to join our team and help shape the future of digital process automation.




Position Overview

We are looking for skilled Business Process Modelers / Business Analysts with hands-on experience in BPMN and/or DMN.

In this role, you will work on analyzing, designing, and improving business processes and decision logic using standard modeling techniques. Senior candidates with strong expertise and experience in leading process improvement initiatives are encouraged to apply.




Key Responsibilities

Develop and maintain business process models using BPMN
Design and implement decision models and business rules using DMN
Work closely with stakeholders and teams to gather and validate requirements
Perform gap analysis and identify process improvement opportunities
Recommend efficient and scalable solutions
Ensure models follow organizational standards and quality guidelines
Support automation and digital transformation initiatives
Participate in workshops and present solutions to stakeholders
Mentor junior team members (for senior roles)




Required Skills & Qualifications

Hands-on experience in BPMN and/or DMN modeling
Strong understanding of business analysis and process improvement (Lean, Six Sigma is a plus)
Good analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
Ability to work independently and in a team environment
Good written and spoken English




Experience

Open to candidates with relevant experience
5+ years of experience preferred for senior roles

title: Business Analyst
description:
Role Overview

We are seeking a Business Analyst to join our product engineering team. This role is embedded within technology development and works closely with Engineering, Product Management, QA, and Operations to design, refine, and deliver scalable workflow-driven solutions.

The ideal candidate brings strong analytical depth, technical credibility, and hands-on experience working within complex, rule-based SaaS systems.

Core Responsibilities


Translate business and operational needs into clear, implementable user stories and acceptance criteria
Model end-to-end workflows, including exception handling and state transitions
Collaborate closely with Engineering during design and build cycles
Validate functionality using data and system behavior
Support production triage and root cause analysis
Drive clarity in ambiguous problem spaces and challenge incomplete requirements
Mentor junior BAs where applicable


Mandatory Skills & Experience

Technical & Analytical


Hands-on experience writing and executing SQL queries for data validation and analysis
Strong understanding of system workflows and backend data flows
Ability to independently analyze production issues and support root cause investigation
Experience working within Agile product development teams


Product & Workflow Design


Experience collaborating on UI designs using Figma or similar tools
Ability to create detailed process maps, user journeys, and workflow documentation
Strong experience defining business rules and configuration logic


Delivery & Ownership


Writes clear, testable user stories with structured acceptance criteria
Breaks down epics into implementable increments
Participates actively in backlog refinement, sprint planning, and demos
Drives UAT coordination and validation
3+ years of Business Analyst experience in product or technology development environments
Experience working in SaaS or enterprise workflow-driven systems


Healthcare Experience Required


2+ years of experience in US Healthcare technology, preferably within claims processing, submissions workflows, eligibility, or revenue cycle systems
Strong understanding of the US claim lifecycle, including submission flows, payer interactions, rejections, and exception handling
Experience working with systems that support payer-facing workflows, clearinghouse interactions, or regulated healthcare transaction processing
Familiarity with US healthcare operational and compliance considerations impacting submissions and workflow design


Good to Have


Understanding of REST APIs and JSON payload structures
Experience documenting integration flows or data mappings
Experience in healthcare technology or regulated industries
Familiarity with batch processing or event-driven systems
Experience with reporting or dashboard tools
Exposure to operational KPI definition and performance metrics
Experience mentoring or leading other analysts
Familiarity with tools such as Postman, Azure DevOps, JIRA, Confluence


Benefits

Veradigm believes in empowering our associates with the tools and flexibility to bring the best version of themselves to work. Through our generous benefits package with an emphasis on work/life balance, we give our employees the opportunity to allow their careers to flourish.


Quarterly Company-Wide Recharge Days

Tuition Reimbursement Program

title: P2P Senior Associate
description:
Job Description



The P2P Senior Associate is responsible for accurate and timely execution of Accounts Payable invoice processing (PO and Non-PO) with strong exception handling, control compliance, and first-line governance.



The role focuses on validating invoice accuracy (tax, coding, approvals), resolving discrepancies, supporting GR/IR and open-item follow-ups, and contributing to month-end close readiness, aging reduction, and audit compliance, while acting as a key escalation point for complex transactions.



Key Responsibilities:



🔹 Invoice Processing & Validation



Process PO and Non-PO invoices in SAP with high accuracy and timeliness



Perform detailed validation of tax, coding, approvals, and duplicate checks



Ensure correct execution of 3-way match (PO, Invoice, GR)



Deliver strong first-time-right (FTR) performance



🔹 Exception Handling & Control Governance



Resolve invoice holds, discrepancies, and complex exceptions



Act as escalation point for issues raised by Junior Associates



Ensure adherence to SOPs, controls, and SOX compliance requirements



Maintain first-line control checks and exception governance



🔹 Reconciliation & Close Support



Support GR/IR reconciliation and open-item follow-ups



Perform vendor statement validation and discrepancy resolution



Contribute to month-end processing, accrual inputs, and close readiness



Ensure completeness of supporting documentation and audit trail



🔹 SLA, Productivity & Queue Governance



Manage workload to meet SLA/KPI targets (accuracy, cycle time, aging)



Maintain and update productivity, aging, and exception trackers



Support queue prioritization and backlog reduction



Ensure timely closure of assigned transactions and exceptions



🔹 RCA, Continuous Improvement & Governance



Identify recurring issues and support root cause analysis (RCA)



Contribute to corrective and preventive actions (CAPA)



Support standardization and continuous improvement initiatives



Highlight risks, inefficiencies, and control gaps



🔹 Stakeholder & Query Management



Coordinate with procurement, requestors, finance/controllers, and treasury



Handle vendor queries related to discrepancies and statement follow-ups



Provide timely and accurate updates on issue resolution



Support knowledge sharing and guidance to P2P associates (non-people management)



🔷 Outcomes (Success Measures)



High invoice accuracy and productivity metrics



Complete exception resolution log with closure status



Timely GR/IR follow-up tracker with resolution timelines



Full audit-ready approval evidence and documentation trail



Measurable aging reduction and backlog improvement



Documented vendor query resolution trail (emails, tickets, workflow notes)





Qualifications



🎓 Education & Experience



Bachelor’s degree in finance, Accounting, or related field



3–6 years of experience in P2P / Accounts Payable operations



🧠 Functional Expertise



Strong hands-on experience in:



Invoice processing (PO & Non-PO)



3-way matching and exception handling



AP controls and compliance (SOX awareness)



GR/IR and vendor reconciliation basics



⚙️ Technical Skills



Experience with SAP (invoice processing, GR/IR visibility)



Familiarity with AP workflow / OCR / portal tools



Proficiency in Excel





Additional Information



Our Benefits



Flexible working environment
Volunteer time off
LinkedIn Learning
Employee-Assistance-Program (EAP)

All use of AI is governed by NIQ’s principles of fairness, transparency, human oversight, and inclusion. Final hiring decisions are made exclusively by humans. Junior Estimation Contract Engineer

Job Summary

As a Junior Estimation Contract Engineer, I assist in preparing accurate cost estimates, quantity take-offs, and budget analysis for residential and commercial construction projects. Prepare quantity take-offs from architectural and structural drawings.

Proficiency in MS Excel (advanced formulas preferred)

Experience with ERP systems (Highrise, StockUp)
Attention to detail and documentation management
Good coordination and communication skills

Bachelor’s Degree / Diploma in Civil Engineering or a related field
Basic knowledge of construction methods, materials, and estimation techniques
Ability to read and interpret architectural and structural drawings
Understanding of BOQ (Bill of Quantities), rate analysis, and cost estimation
Familiarity with Bar Bending Schedule (BBS) preparation
Proficiency in MS Excel, AutoCAD, and basic ERP systems (e.g., Highrise, StockUp)
Knowledge of GFC drawings and quantity take-off methods
Strong analytical, numerical, and problem-solving skills
Good communication and coordination abilities

title: Junior Copywriter
description:
Job Summary


As a copywriter, you'll work alongside the art director within the creative department. You'll work from client briefs to conceive, develop and produce effective advertising campaigns. You'll focus on providing the written words (the copy) for an advertising campaign.
Copy can include: Creating headlines, slogans, catchphrases, straplines and body copy for print advertising and leaflets writing for web advertising, social media and mobile applications, creating scripts for radio jingles and TV commercials.
You'll also work with media planners/media buyers and the production department to fully develop an advertising campaign


Role & Responsibilities


Work in partnership with an art director to form a creative team working on creative briefs
Knowledge about real estate advertisement
Develop & Manage content strategists and oversee all content creation activities for the clients.
Familiarize yourself with your clients' products and services, the target audience and their competitors' activities.
Develop innovative strategies for clients basis their overall brand strategy and goals.
Write clear, persuasive, original copy through the various iterations of the project.
Proofread copy to check spelling and grammar
Ensure monthly calendars detailing strategy for clients as well as internal briefs for the creative/photography/ ATL/ Digital ( etc )teams are shared in a timely manner.
Collaborate with our internal teams to manage the planning, creation, and delivery of original content.
Amend, revise or redevelop adverts or campaigns in response to feedback from the creative director, account team or clients
Stay up-to-date with current technologies and trends in social media, design tools and applications
Monitor the effectiveness of advertising campaigns.


Qualification


Strategic thinking and brand understanding
Strong analytical skills
Hands-on experience in social media marketing and content development
Excellent teamwork, communication and interpersonal skills
Previous knowledge or background in copywriting is a must
Logic, creativity and imagination
Good research skills
Commercial awareness with the ability to understand the target audience
Previous experience with Instagram & Facebook marketing is a plus
Understanding of aesthetic
Experience in/around a startup culture is a plus- i.e. you can adapt to quick changes within the company
Exceptionally detail-oriented and organized
The ability to write good, clear copy in a variety of styles, with accurate spelling and grammar
The ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines

title: Junior Software Engineer
description:
Your Role

“..start your journey as a thoughtful software engineer today and grow into an AI‑capable developer of tomorrow…”

As a Junior Software Engineer, you will help implement backend services and integration components that support our enterprise applications. Working closely with Senior Engineers and Staff Engineers you’ll assist in shaping solutions, participating in design reviews, and learning best‑practice engineering standards.

You’ll collaborate with experienced front‑end and back‑end developers, contributing to complete features rather than isolated tasks as you gradually take on more responsibility. This role gives you hands-on experience with end‑to‑end product development and encourages you to build strong engineering fundamentals.

Operating in a multinational environment, you’ll gain exposure to diverse stakeholders and systems where reliability, maintainability, and long‑term quality are essential. You will also have opportunities to explore practical applications of GenAI that enhance developer productivity and support broader business efficiency.

What You Will Work On


Contributing to backend services and APIs for core enterprise systems (e.g., product information management, ERP and e‑commerce integrations, workflow automations)
Assisting in developing event‑driven integrations across platforms
Supporting the creation of reliable components designed for long‑term use
Participating in continuous improvement of development workflows, architecture patterns, and engineering guidelines
Exploring practical applications of GenAI, such as RAG‑based retrieval or vector search, under guidance from senior engineers
Contribution to GenAI use cases such as RAG-based information retrieval or vector search


Main Responsibilities

Engineering & Architecture (Entry Level)


Implement backend services and REST APIs using modern .NET following established patterns and guidance.
Work with Senior and Staff Engineers to understand solution architectures and contribute to technical discussions.
Apply clean code principles, basic automated testing, and maintain clear documentation.
Contribute to long‑term system maintainability through careful implementation and attention to quality.


AI‑Enhanced Development


Learn and adopt AI‑assisted development tools and practices to improve productivity.
Support the team in experimenting with GenAI where appropriate.


Collaboration & Growth


Work closely with engineers across multiple countries and cultures.
Ask questions, seek guidance, and actively learn from more experienced developers.
Participate in code reviews, share learnings, and contribute to a supportive engineering culture.
Collaborate with product owners and domain experts to clarify requirements and deliver high‑quality features.


Required

To succeed, you will need:


Education: Master’s degree in computer science or equivalent
3-4 years of professional experience in backend/software engineering
Foundational knowledge of .NET, C#, and ASP.NET Web API
Basic understanding of Azure (e.g., App Services, identity concepts, DevOps pipelines) with motivation to learn more
Basic understanding of Azure (e.g., App Services, identity concepts, DevOps pipelines) with motivation to learn more
Familiarity with databases such as MongoDB, CosmosDB, or PostgreSQL
Interest in event‑driven architecture and modern integration patterns
Motivation to use AI‑assisted coding tools and grow your skills in AI‑augmented development
Willingness to work in large, international teams and collaborate across cultures
Comfort in agile, fast‑moving, and innovative environments where continuous learning is encouraged


Nice to Have


Exposure to the product development lifecycle
Experience working in agile product teams (Scrum, Kanban, etc.)
Basic functional analysis skills (e.g., Use cases, UML)
Curiosity about vector databases, embeddings, or GenAI concepts (e.g., RAG, knowledge graphs)
Experience with other programming languages such as Node.js, Python, or TypeScript


Why Join Us


Work on central enterprise systems with real business impact
Modern technology stack and forward‑looking architecture
Strong engineering culture with high autonomy and ownership
Opportunity to shape AI‑supported software development practices
Hybrid working model with remote options across the EU
Collaborative, international environment centered on technical excellence
Choose your career with us Drive your Career, Explore Opportunities, Realize your Passion . . . We at Atlas Copco believe that there is always a better way. This spirit drives us to provide leading edge technology, innovative and sustainable products, as well as best-in-class service to our customers around the globe. That’s where you


Uniting curious minds

Behind every innovative solution, there are people working together to transform the future. With careers sparked by initiative and lifelong learning, we unite curious minds, and you could be one of them.

title: Junior Engineer II, Plant Maintenance
description:
Introduction: A Career at HARMAN Automotive

We’re a global, multi-disciplinary team that’s putting the innovative power of technology to work and transforming tomorrow. At HARMAN Automotive, we give you the keys to fast-track your career.


Engineer audio systems and integrated technology platforms that augment the driving experience.
Combine ingenuity, in-depth research, and a spirit of collaboration with design and engineering excellence.
Advance in-vehicle infotainment, safety, efficiency, and enjoyment


About The Role

The position is expected to be located at Plant. Facilities management - Plans, execute and coordinates the pm activities concerned with equipment selection, installation and commissioning, construction, modification, and maintenance of equipment machinery in industrial plant.

What You Will Do


Operations and Maintenance of utilities systems - HT & LT Systems, UPS systems, HVAC, Air compressors, DG Sets, ups, Nitrogen to support to Production function.
Maintenance of Matl handling equipment’s
FPS Systems, Access controls and CCTV management
Coordination with Sub-contractors for Execution
Execution of New Projects/ Layout Change, New Equipment Installation, MSEB Licensing Statutory compliances
Enhance safety Environment and implementation of safety process / systems
Maintain the KPI - MTTR, MTBF, 3C, 5S
Spares Management
Energy management
Implementing Certification (OSHAS, TS 16949, ISO 50001), EMS, OEM’s Specific certifications
EMS & Haz management systems,
Statutory compliance


What You Need To Be Successful


Diploma / BE in Electrical Engineering 5 to 7 years of experience in Plant engineering function / Utilities systems operation and maintenance
Certification / License: C license / Supervisor License – Issued by Electricity board.


What Makes You Eligible


Be willing to travel up to domestic/International travel.
Be willing to work in office based on up dependencies.


What We Offer


Flexible work environment, allowing for full-time remote work globally for positions that can be performed outside a HARMAN or customer location.
Access to employee discounts on world-class Harman and Samsung products (JBL, HARMAN Kardon, AKG, etc.)
Extensive training opportunities through our own HARMAN University
Competitive wellness benefits
Tuition reimbursement
“Be Brilliant” employee recognition and rewards program.
An inclusive and diverse work environment that fosters and encourages professional and personal development.


You Belong Here

HARMAN is committed to making every employee feel welcomed, valued, and empowered. No matter what role you play, we encourage you to share your ideas, voice your distinct perspective, and bring your whole self with you – all within a support-minded culture that celebrates what makes each of us unique. We also recognize that learning is a lifelong pursuit and want you to flourish. We proudly offer added opportunities for training, development, and continuing education, further empowering you to live the career you want.

About HARMAN: Where Innovation Unleashes Next-Level Technology

Ever since the 1920s, we’ve been amplifying the sense of sound. Today, that legacy endures, with integrated technology platforms that make the world smarter, safer, and more connected.

Across automotive, lifestyle, and digital transformation solutions, we create innovative technologies that turn ordinary moments into extraordinary experiences. Our renowned automotive and lifestyle solutions can be found everywhere, from the music we play in our cars and homes to venues that feature today’s most sought-after performers, while our digital transformation solutions serve humanity by addressing the world’s ever-evolving needs and demands. Marketing our award-winning portfolio under 16 iconic brands, such as JBL, Mark Levinson, and Revel, we set ourselves apart by exceeding the highest engineering and design standards for our customers, our partners and each other.

If you’re ready to innovate and do work that makes a lasting impact, join our talent community today!

Important Notice: Recruitment Scams

Please be aware that HARMAN recruiters will always communicate with you from an '@harman.com', ‘@careers.harman.com’ or ‘harmanglobal.avature.net’ email address. We will never ask for payments, banking, credit card, personal financial information or access to your LinkedIn/email account during the screening, interview, or recruitment process. If you are asked for such information or receive communication from an email address not ending in one of the above email domains about a job with HARMAN, please cease communication immediately and report the incident to us through: harmancareers@harman.com.

HARMAN is proud to be an Equal Opportunity employer. HARMAN strives to hire the best qualified candidates and is committed to building a workforce representative of the diverse marketplaces and communities of our global colleagues and customers. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. HARMAN attracts, hires, and develops employees based on merit, qualifications and job-related performance.(www.harman.com)

title: Engineer
description:
We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Junior Engineer to join our Railway Design Team, specializing in alignment, track, and structural engineering. The role involves assisting in the preparation of railway alignment designs, geometric layouts, trackwork planning, and structural detailing under the guidance of senior engineers. Responsibilities include developing CAD drawings, preparing design reports, coordinating with multidisciplinary teams, supporting site teams with design clarifications, and ensuring compliance with railway standards (Indian Railways, RDSO, AREMA, UIC, or FIDIC). The candidate should possess a basic understanding of the design of civil structures such as bridges, viaducts, and culverts, along with knowledge of geotechnical investigations (GTI) and embankment design for railway formation. Exposure to slab track and ballasted track systems, drainage concepts, and interface requirements will be an added advantage. Strong analytical skills, proficiency in design software (AutoCAD, Civil 3D, STAAD, or similar), and the ability to interpret technical specifications are essential. This role provides excellent opportunities to learn advanced railway engineering practices, gain hands-on project experience, and build a strong foundation for a career in large-scale rail and metro infrastructure projects.

title: Analyst/Senior Analyst (TMT)
description:
Location

New Delhi

Job Description

Job title: Analyst/Senior Analyst (TMT)

Company Description

We’re L.E.K. Consulting, a global strategy consultancy working with business leaders to seize competitive advantage and amplify growth. Our insights are catalysts that reshape the trajectory of our clients’ businesses, uncovering opportunities and empowering them to master their moments of truth. Since 1983, our worldwide practice—spanning the Americas, Asia Pacific, and Europe—has guided leaders across all industries, from global corporations to emerging entrepreneurial businesses and private equity investors. Looking for more? Visit

Position Overview

The L.E.K. Capability Network (LCN) is an integrated part of L.E.K. Consulting, collaborating closely with consulting teams in L.E.K.’s worldwide network of offices to support them on their toughest and most impactful assignments.

The Analyst / Senior Analyst at LCN plays a critical role in supporting case teams by leading analytical modules, managing research initiatives, and providing insightful recommendations.

A typical project might involve an in-depth primary research campaign to identify and triage potential experts to speak to in a particular field, before holding discussions with them to discover their insights on a topic. It could involve rigorous secondary research to prove or disprove a client hypothesis, leveraging the full arsenal of L.E.K. toolkits and databases. Or it could mean undertaking a critical analysis to illuminate a pattern from the noise around it, to substantiate a strategic decision. They are expected to take greater ownership of their work, proactively structure analysis, and effectively communicate findings. This role requires strong problem-solving skills, the ability to manage multiple tasks across time zones, and a collaborative mindset to mentor junior analysts and contribute to L.E.K.'s culture. More information can be found at

We are currently hiring for Analyst/Senior Analysts into the LCN, a role which will report into the LCN Team lead/Team manager

Primary Research

Responsibilities will include, but are not limited to:


Manage interview campaign including creating IV guide, expert identification, managing expert networks and other vendors, and manage real-time update
Independently work with consulting staff on projects by sourcing content experts and key opinion leaders (e.g., with LinkedIn, expert networks, cold calling, etc.)
Write solicitations for experts to conduct interviews with case teams, and custom solicitations for key opinion leaders (e.g., referring to papers he / she has published)
Conduct local / international interview campaigns and summarize key takeaways from the interview to help case teams derive strategic conclusions
Extract and curate key insights from interviews to validate or challenge case hypotheses, driving informed decision-making


Secondary research


Own end-to-end secondary research modules, identifying relevant data sources and extracting key insights
Collaborate with teams to identify secondary research resources that address client areas of interest (e.g., journal articles, marketing / industry reports, government publications)
Confirm/disprove case team hypotheses, deriving insights and patterns from unstructured problem statements using publicly available reports, articles, thought pieces, company filings and GenAI
Independently contribute to case teams by applying structured problem-solving techniques to test and validate case hypotheses


Analysis: Modelling / surveys


Manage and deliver end-to-end survey analysis including coding, testing, analysis and curating insights
Co-develop comprehensive models such as market models, revenue models, and cost models to support various due-diligence cases
Utilize Excel, Alteryx, Tableau, and other analytical tools to structure data and create compelling visualizations
Review client data to understand gaps in datasets


Slide preparation and quality control


Design and recommend analytical frameworks to effectively present insights in a clear, accurate, and impactful manner
Develop clear and compelling commentary that extracts actionable insights
Create sophisticated visuals in PowerPoint and Tableau for consulting teams to use in client presentations
Collaborate with L.E.K. Graphic Design team to recommend visually interesting layouts for showing complex data
Review slide formatting for quality control, making layouts consistent and in line with L.E.K. brand standards (e.g., updating L.E.K. IP to current visual standards)


Coaching and mentorship


Provide support and oversight to junior analysts on projects, ensuring they meet project objectives
Coach junior analysts on technical skillsets such as secondary research using databases, effective slide writing and data analysis
Share industry insights and best practices with team members to enhance their skills and knowledge.


Case closure support


Prepare redacted versions of materials for delivery to KM
Prepare PowerPoint release documents for case team to deliver to client
Facilitate access agreements process for teams interacting with investors (e.g., as part of a due diligence)


Qualifications


The ideal candidate will have 3-5 years of relevant experience with a top-tier consulting firm
An undergraduate degree is required
Fair understanding of business concepts and their practical applications
Ability to work on multiple tasks and activities at once and flexibility to re-prioritize
Expert PowerPoint and Excel skills
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal
High attention to detail


Benefits And Perks


L.E.K. offers you the opportunity to work in a hybrid setup
We are among the best paying firms globally
Generous time away
We have Parental leaves (Maternity and Paternity) and flexible work options for new mothers
We provide childcare facilities in Mumbai (Day care / Creche services).
Wellness benefits – Health Insurance, Wellness Apps, Gym reimbursement, etc.

description:
Ways of Working - Remote - Employees will have the freedom to work remotely all through the year. These employees, who form a large majority, will come together in their base location (Bangalore) for a week, once every quarter.




About Swiggy

Swiggy is India’s leading on-demand delivery platform with a tech-first approach to logistics and a solution-first approach to consumer demands. With a presence in 500+ cities across India, partnerships with hundreds of thousands of restaurants, an employee base of over 5000, and a 2 lakh+ strong independent fleet of Delivery Executives, we deliver unparalleled convenience driven by continuous innovation. Built on the back of robust ML technology and fueled by terabytes of data processed every day, Swiggy offers a fast, seamless, and reliable delivery experience for millions of customers across India. From starting out as a hyperlocal food delivery service in 2014 to becoming India’s leading on-demand convenience platform today, our capabilities result not only in lightning-fast delivery for customers but also in a productive and fulfilling experience for our employees.




Roles & Responsibilities

Write clear, compelling, and engaging copy for multiple channels including brand films, performance marketing, social media, in-app channels and delivery partner communications, ensuring alignment with Swiggy’s tone and voice.
Develop innovative and creative concepts and messaging that resonate with the delivery partner audience and reflect campaign objectives.
Collaborate closely with the creative team, designers, and marketers to produce integrated content that enhances brand storytelling and user engagement.
Edit, proofread, and elevate copy produced by junior writers to maintain high editorial standards and consistency.
Translate marketing briefs into powerful written communication that inspires and motivates delivery partners.
Maintain and evolve a consistent brand voice specifically tailored to delivery partners across all communications.
Use insights and feedback to refine copy for improved impact and effectiveness.
Keep abreast of industry trends and best practices to inject fresh ideas into the marketing strategy.
Contribute hands-on to content creation while mentoring junior copywriters to grow their skills and craft.




Desired Skills & Experience

Experience as a copywriter, preferably with exposure to brand marketing, and specially social media marketing or advertising agency environments.
Strong portfolio showcasing a range of creative work across diverse platforms and formats, with emphasis on user-focused, result-driven writing.
Exceptional writing, editing, and storytelling skills with impeccable attention to detail.
Ability to convey complex messages in simple, relatable language that speaks directly to delivery partners.
Aptitude for collaborating with design and marketing teams to create cohesive campaign assets.
Understanding of digital marketing dynamics, including social, performance, and brand communications.
Proactive attitude with the ability to work independently and manage multiple deadlines while maintaining quality.
A mindset that values creativity as an evolving process defined by audience connection, clarity, and impact.

title: Data Analyst
description:
The Role

Reporting to the Global Manager, Data Access Control, the Data Access Control Analyst is the driver of all technical aspects of the Data Governance team. The Data Access Analyst will apply all rules and associated policies and procedures of the Data Governance process to all user and data access requests. The Data Access Analyst should be customer focused and impacts organisational performance on processes related to client data. This individual should apply their technical skills to execute the Data Governance framework and associated policies and procedures. The individual will also be building ETL pipelines and developing dashboards for auditing and analytical purposes to ensure our data remains governed.




Key Responsibilities

Data Engineering & ETL: Build and automate robust data pipelines in Domo using Magic ETL and Python. You’ll integrate and consolidate metadata from multiple instances to ensure high-quality data accessibility for reporting and auditing..
Advanced Visualization: Design, build, and maintain sophisticated dashboards in Domo, Power BI, or Tableau that provide "at-a-glance" clarity for executive stakeholders.
Data Governance: Act as a steward for data integrity. You’ll implement governance standards to ensure data consistency, security, and accuracy across the organization.
Strategic Analysis: Perform deep-dive investigations into trends and variances, turning raw numbers into narratives that support our long-term strategic initiatives.
Modernization & Mentorship: Lead the shift toward automated reporting. You’ll educate junior team members on new tools and best practices in Python and BI.

What You Bring

Technical Excellence: Strong proficiency in Python (Pandas, NumPy) and advanced SQL.
BI Mastery: Extensive experience with at least one major BI tool (Domo, Power BI, or Tableau).
Analytical Mindset: You can solve complex problems independently by critical thinking and are comfortable navigating "grey areas" where precedents might not exist.
Governance Exposure: A solid understanding of data lineage, metadata management, and data quality frameworks.
Data Quality:Monitor and track data quality issues, identify gaps, and apply action plans to remediate gap
Communication: The ability to explain complex technical insights to non-technical audiences and influence decision-making.

The Junior Full Stack Developer contributes to the design, development, and maintenance of internal business applications used across Epiroc. The role supports product teams in building secure, scalable, and user‑friendly digital solutions for sourcing applications and collaborates closely with senior developers to ensure high‑quality delivery.

Software Development


Develop and maintain web applications across the full stack using modern frameworks and tools.

Build and maintain ASP.NET / C# back‑end services and REST APIs.

Integrate secure authentication and authorization using Azure AD / SSO (OAuth2/OIDC, tokens, scopes, consent).

Contribute to application and infrastructure automation using Azure and Infrastructure‑as‑Code practices.

Experience

2–3 years of hands‑on software development in a full‑stack or front‑end focused role.

Back‑end: ASP.NET / C#, REST API development.

Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, Software Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.


It all starts with people. The world needs metals and minerals for the energy transition and our cities and infrastructure must be developed to serve a growing population. To succeed, we need to speed up the shift towards more sustainable mining and construction industries. We at Epiroc accelerate this transformation, together with customers and business partners in more than 150 countries, by developing and providing innovative and safe equipment, digital solutions, and aftermarket support.

All new thinkers are welcome. We are looking for those who want to develop, grow, and dare to think new. In Epiroc we attract, develop, and retain diverse talent valuing authenticity and unique perspectives, driving our spirit of innovation. We foster an inclusive culture where diversity isn't just a goal but a part of our values and way of working. This is how we do business for a sustainable future.  Learn more at www.epiroc.com

title: Seller Operations Executive
description:
About noon

We’re building an ecosystem of digital products and services that power everyday life across the Middle East—fast, scalable, and deeply customer-centric. Our mission is to deliver to every door every day. We want to redefine what technology can do in this region, and we’re looking for a Seller Operator (Operations) who can help us move even faster.




Our mission: Ring every doorbell, every day.




What you'll do:

At noon, you’ll work alongside some of the fastest, smartest, and most driven people in the region. Our teams are young, ambitious, and relentlessly execution-focused, working on high-impact initiatives every day.




Overview of the role:

Looking for a dynamic Operations Associate to support forward logistics, reverse operations, and seller onboarding. The role involves managing order flows, handling returns, and assisting sellers to ensure smooth marketplace operations. Ideal for candidates who are detail-oriented, eager to learn, and thrive in a fast-paced environment.




What you'll need:

1. Forward Operations:

Assist in the day-to-day execution of forward logistics operations.
Support order processing activities to ensure accuracy and efficiency.
Collaborate with team members to maintain optimal inventory levels and smooth shipment processes.
Learn and contribute to the optimization of supply chain operations.




2. Reverse Operations:

Support the management of the reverse logistics process, including returns and exchanges.
Assist in the implementation of effective return policies to minimize costs and enhance customer satisfaction.
Analyze return trends under guidance to provide insights for process improvement.
Work closely with the team to address and resolve return-related inquiries.




3. Seller Onboarding:

Contribute to the onboarding process for new sellers onto our platform.
Assist in maintaining up-to-date onboarding documentation for sellers.
Provide support in training sessions for sellers and act as a point of contact for basic inquiries.
Collaborate with team members to streamline and enhance the overall onboarding process.




Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration or equivalent practical experience.
Fluent in English with excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Entry-level candidates or 1-3 years of relevant work experience. -Strong organizational skills with attention to detail. -Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Ability to work effectively in a collaborative team environment. -Eagerness to learn and contribute to process improvement.




Who will excel?

We’re looking for candidates who thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic start-up environment.
We’re searching for problem solvers, people who operate with a bias for action and have a deep understanding of the importance of resourcefulness over reliance.
Candor is our only default. Demanding unequivocal high standards should be non-negotiable because quality matters.
We want people who are radically candid, cohorts who commit to settling for nothing but the best - in hiring, in accepting work from colleagues, and in your own work. Ours is not an easy mission, but it is a meaningful one.
Every hire must actively raise the bar of talent in the company to help us reach our vision.

We are recruiting on behalf of our client, one of the bulge bracket bank and pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices on an ongoing basis through the first half of each year, with the view of onboarding immediately or upon graduation.




Our client is looking for two professionals to join their Investment Banking team in Mumbai as an Analyst and use their skills and ideas to make an impact on the success of the bank. You will either be placed in their FIG, TMT or Energy Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry including but not limited to Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.




The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.







Responsibilities

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates
Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options
Prepare client-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation
Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution
Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines
Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders
Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work
Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams




This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.




You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.







Minimum Qualifications




Graduate – Analyst

Bachelor’s degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client-ready materials
High attention to detail and strong organisational skills
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations
We consider any degree or subject and although your grade indicates your ability to absorb data and apply it, your degree grade does not influence your chance of applying to us and being interviewed.







Process

This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets.




Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.







Reward Structure

Analyst annual base salary of 125,000 and up to 150,000 United States Dollars depending on experience. Your salary will be paid in Indian Rupees and bonus in USD
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high-achievers
Relocation is provided by the company

title: Guest Relations Executive (Management Training Program)
description:
Join Everest Base Camp Mussoorie:

Management Trainee Program

YOUR OPPORTUNITY TO LEAD THE NEXT CHAPTER

Are you a supervisory professional ready to level up? Do you have the hunger to grow, the

drive to lead, and the vision to shape the future of an exciting hospitality venture? Everest

Base Camp (EBC), luxury camping resort in the heart of Mussoorie, is looking for

two exceptional Management Trainees to join our rapidly expanding team.




ABOUT EVEREST BASE CAMP

Nestled in the untouched landscape near Sir George Everest House, EBC Mussoorie is a

premium luxury glamping resort redening hospitality in the Himalayan foothills. With

our iconic plush tents, world-class amenities, curated experiences, and our signature Park

Cafe, we're creating magical memories for guests from across India and beyond.

We're growing fast. And we need emerging leaders like you to grow with us.




THE ROLE: MANAGEMENT TRAINEE

program for supervisory professionals who are ready to evolve.

relations, F&B operations, and housekeeping functions

Guest Experience Excellence: Ensure every guest interaction reects our

commitment to premium hospitality and creates unforgettable moments

Team Development & Mentorship: Coach and develop sta, building high-

performance teams while learning modern leadership practices

Operational Eciency: Implement best practices, optimize processes, and

contribute to P&L management

Strategic Growth Initiatives: Participate in property expansion plans and help

establish systems for our upcoming campus growth

Training & Quality Assurance: Conduct training sessions, maintain service

standards, and drive continuous improvement

Stakeholder Management: Build relationships with guests, vendors, and internal

teams to ensure seamless operations.




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Your Background:

2-5+ years of supervisory/management experience in hospitality (Front Oce

Supervisor , Guest Relations Supervisor , F&B Supervisor , or similar roles)

Proven track record of leading teams and delivering exceptional guest experiences

Strong operational and problem-solving skills with attention to detail

Understanding of hospitality management fundamentals

Your Qualities:

Ambitious: Hungry to grow into a leadership role and make an impact

Adaptable: Thriving in a fast-paced, growing property with evolving challenges

Guest-Centric: Passionate about creating memorable experiences

Collaborative: Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

Proactive: Willing to take ownership and drive initiatives

Resilient: Ready for the dynamic nature of hospitality operations

Nice-to-Have:

Bachelor's degree in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or related

eld

Experience with hotel management systems (PMS) and POS.




WHY JOIN EBC ?

Fast-Track Leadership Development

You won't just manage—you'll learn to lead. Our structured mentorship program

accelerates your growth into future leadership roles

Real responsibility in a real, growing property with direct visibility to senior

management

Opportunities to shape operations and contribute to strategic decisions

Be part of something exciting and new: EBC is in its growth phase with expansion

plans and thrilling possibilities

Join an Up-and-Coming Property

Build the culture and systems from the ground up—your input shapes our future

Work in a stunning natural setting that inspires guests and energizes teams




Comprehensive Professional Development

Formal training programs in leadership, operations, and guests and energizes teams

Comprehensive Professional Development

Formal training programs in leadership, operations, and guest service excellence

Mentorship from experienced hospitality professionals

Exposure to multi-property operations and best practices

Clear pathway to Rooms Division Manager and F&B Manager roles.




THE MUSSOORIE ADVANTAGE

Mussoorie is one of India's most beautiful hill stations. Live and work surrounded by

breathtaking Himalayan views, curated experiences, trekking opportunities, and a vibrant

hospitality ecosystem. This isn't just a job location—it's a lifestyle upgrade.




This isn't a position for everyone. We're looking for supervisors who've realized they have

what it takes to lead. If you're tired of doing the same thing and ready to grow, to challenge

yourself, and to be part of building something exciting in the mountains of Mussoorie—

then Everest Base Camp needs you.

Your next chapter starts here.

Our institutional clients comprise over 1,000 universities, colleges, and biopharma companies, including such leaders as Harvard, MIT, Yale, and Stanford. The Role

Assisting Editors by developing organizational structures
Compiling manuscripts and abstracts to form method collections
Data entry and CRM management

Contributing to special projects to increase team performance

Excellent written and verbal communication, including strong English grammar skills
Ability to work independently once given a project

Why Join JoVE?

title: Data Processing Executive
description:
Your primary role:

The Data Processing Executive role will report into The Data Processing Manager position. The Data Processing Executive will be a key member of the newly formed India-based Data Processing Hub, supporting quantitative healthcare market research projects across global markets. This role is responsible for the accurate and timely production of tabulations, both interim and final, and requires close collaboration with research, project management, and scripting teams. This will include:


Running and validating interim and final tabulations (uncoded and coded) for quantitative research studies using relevant software (e.g., Q, Quantum, SPSS, Excel-based platforms, or proprietary tools).
Ensuring all tabulations meet internal and client-specific formatting, logic, and output requirements.
Checking tabulated data for accuracy, completeness, and consistency before delivery.
Supporting QA processes and documentation to maintaining high data standards across projects.
Assisting in preparing raw datasets for processing, including basic cleaning, formatting, and consistency checks.
Working with coded and uncoded datasets, ensuring proper integration into final tabulation outputs.
Liaising with scripting/programming team to ensure accurate data structure for tabulation.
Working closely with the research and project management teams to understand project requirements, timelines, and deliverables.
Providing input and feedback to internal stakeholders to optimise processes and outputs.
Managing assigned tasks within deadlines and flag any delays or data issues proactively to the manager and wider team.
Maintaining clear documentation of tabulation processes and version control.
Supporting in updating project logs and workflow tracking systems.
Continuously improving knowledge of data processing tools, tabulation best practices, and healthcare market research processes.


About You…

Your skills and experience:


More than 2 years of experience in a data processing within a market research agency (ideally healthcare but can be other market research sectors)
Basic to intermediate proficiency in data tabulation software (preferably Q, but Quantum or similar will be considered as well)
Exposure to or understanding of survey scripting tools and survey data structures is a plus
Experience working with survey data formats (e.g., XML, CSV), as well as knowledge of relational databases and data structure management.
Understanding of quantitative research methodologies, questionnaire structures, and healthcare market research best practices.
Demonstrable experience of working with senior team members
Problem-solving skills (particularly relating to situations requiring analytical judgement and establishing best practice solutions)
Excellent analytical and numerical skills
Strong communication skills, particularly in the ability to explain technical points to non-technical people in an international environment
High attention to detail with a strong focus on data accuracy and consistency.
Logical thinking and ability to troubleshoot errors or discrepancies in tabulated data.
Ability to interpret tabulation specifications and apply them accurately.


Ideal profile:


You are proactive, dedicated and enthusiastic, with a ‘can do’ attitude and approach
You demonstrate a high level of accuracy and attention to detail, as well as strengths in organisation and prioritisation
You are a collaborative team player who works well with colleagues
You communicate ideas and issues in an effective, straightforward fashion
You are highly motivated and want to seize opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career


Life at Research Partnership:

We love what we do and continuously strive for innovation and creativity. We believe everyone should continuously be learning and provide:


A comprehensive training and development programme for all staff, from entry level apprentice to senior management
Opportunities for international travel and collaboration
A relaxed and friendly working environment


About Inizio Advisory:

Research Partnership is part of Inizio Advisory, a trusted advisor for pharmaceutical and life science companies that turns science into strategy, supporting execution at every stage of the product and patient journey from early commercialization to launch and beyond. Offering market research and insights; strategy; people and transformation consulting; and commercial benchmarking delivered through sector specific connected solutions and intelligence, Inizio Advisory helps create long-term value for clients and their patients.

Don't meet every job requirement? That's okay! Our company is dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role, but your experience doesn't perfectly fit every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this role or others.

description:
NATS (National Apprenticeship Training Scheme) is our structured Apprenticeship Program aimed at providing hands-on learning opportunities only for fresh graduates / post graduates (Eligible Batch FY'21-FY'25) helping them build industry-ready skills & kick start their career with us.

Through this program, we’re investing in early talent by offering them mentorship & a strong foundation to grow with us .




Role Overview

We are looking for an enthusiastic and detail‐oriented Learning & Development (L&D)

Administrator to support the smooth running of L&D programs. This entry‐level role will focus

Data Management: Collect data from stakeholders and prepare simple dashboards and review decks.

Strong attention to detail and ability to stay organized.

Proactively proposes actionable fixes rather than just reporting problems.

title: Quality Analyst
description:
Location::Bangalore

Sapiens International Corporation (NASDAQ and TASE: SPNS) is a leading global provider of software solutions for the insurance industry, with a growing presence in the financial services sector. We offer integrated core software solutions and business services, and a full digital suite for the property and casualty/general insurance; life, pension, and annuities; and reinsurance markets. Sapiens also services the workers’ compensation and financial and compliance markets. Our portfolio includes policy administration, billing, and claims; underwriting, illustration and electronic application; reinsurance and decision management software. Sapiens’ digital platform features customer and agent portals, and a business intelligence platform. With a 40-year track record of delivering to more than 600 organizations, Sapiens’ team of over 5000 operates through our fully-owned subsidiaries in North America, the United Kingdom, EMEA, and Asia Pacific. For more information: www.sapiens.com .

Job description:-


Plans, designs and executes test cases to confirm that functional test and performance requirements are met
Creates and maintains comprehensive test procedures for client-server application from formal, written business requirements and standards documentation
Experience required: 3 to 4 years.
Performs regression, functional, performance, load, stress, integration tests to ensure product performance and integrity following documented procedures standards
Designs comprehensive test cases for manual testing. Executes system testing within QA environment(s) to validate defect fixes and functional enhancements to the system -- using completed, comprehensive test procedures and supplemental test documentation
Performs black box testing
Performs white box testing and is able to comprehend; navigates within the database table structures
Translates UI and business requirements standards to the system under test
Create entry level SQL queries involving multiple tables within the database structure
Participates in requirements reviews with business analysts, development teams and technical architects to ensure requirements are clear, concise and testable
Uses defect-tracking tool to analyze, report and track application defects and enhancement change requests.


Disclaimer:- Sapiens India does not authorise any third parties to release employment offers or conduct recruitment drives via a third party. Hence, beware of inauthentic and fraudulent job offers or recruitment drives from any individuals or websites purporting to represent Sapiens . Further, Sapiens does not charge any fee or other emoluments for any reason (including without limitation, visa fees) or seek compensation from educational institutions to participate in recruitment events. Accordingly, please check the authenticity of any such offers before acting on them and where acted upon, you do so at your own risk. Sapiens shall neither be responsible for honouring or making good the promises made by fraudulent third parties, nor for any monetary or any other loss incurred by the aggrieved individual or educational institution. In the event that you come across any fraudulent activities in the name of Sapiens , please feel free report the incident at sapiens to sharedservices@sapiens.com

title: Early Careers Recruitment_Associate
description:
Job Description

Build strategic partnerships and a diverse entry-level talent pipeline as a Junior Recruiter in the Early Careers Recruitment team.

Are you ready to shape the future of talent acquisition at JPMorgan Chase? Join our dynamic Early Careers team and be at the forefront of recruiting top-tier junior talent.

As a Campus Recruiter within the Early Careers team, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of our workforce by building a robust pipeline of diverse intern talent across our programs. You will serve as the subject matter expert in early careers recruitment, leveraging your expertise to attract and engage top-tier candidates.

Job Responsibilities


Manages the recruiting process end-to-end and formulates an effective recruiting strategy through partnerships with business stakeholders and the broader Early Careers team.
Builds and owns key relationships with business leaders, program management, and other stakeholders to provide visibility on recruiting activity and collaborate on engagement plans.
Partners internally with other areas of the organization – including Talent Acquisition Marketing and Employer Brand to create a consistent and engaging candidate experience that promotes JPMorgan Chase as an employer of choice.
Owns and executes any diversity-specific initiatives, location-based on country talent acquisition strategy.
Builds and maintains relationships with universities and internal stakeholders and leads the design and delivery of an effective events plan for any in-person and/or virtual activity.
Partners closely with the Early Careers Operations team to facilitate a seamless candidate and business experience in line with all appropriate recruiting risk and controls.
Actively manages all candidates through the pipeline in a timely manner including candidate progression, candidate review, Superday management, offer communications, etc.
Plans and delivers the summer internship program end-to-end; including support and mentorship for interns, performance development, and conversion to the full-time analyst program.
Utilizes data and reporting to identify improvements to existing processes, provides key metrics to stakeholders with a focus on return and outcomes, and actively manages and tracks budgets. 


Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills


3 years plus of Human resources experience and MBA or similar qualification
Ability to work in a team in addition to working independently.
Strong organizational, planning, time-management skills, and the ability to multi-task.
Strategic thinker with strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Project management experience: demonstrated capability to lead and manage projects.
Strong relationship management skills and the ability to interact with all levels of stakeholders.
Effective influencing skills and demonstrated ability to build internal and external relationships.
Proven attention to detail with excellent communication skills: verbally and written.
Flexible and adaptable to a fast-paced work environment.
Exceptional Microsoft Office skills (i.e., Word, PowerPoint, Excel).


Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills


Recruiting experience in volume/early career recruiting
Work experience within global Financial Services  
Experience with candidate sourcing platforms e.g. Handshake, LinkedIn etc.  


Additional Information

This role requires regular travel within India. Join us to make a significant impact on our talent acquisition strategy and help shape the future of our workforce.

About Us

JPMorganChase, one of the oldest financial institutions, offers innovative financial solutions to millions of consumers, small businesses and many of the world’s most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands. Our history spans over 200 years and today we are a leader in investment banking, consumer and small business banking, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management.

We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.

About The Team

Our professionals in our Corporate Functions cover a diverse range of areas from finance and risk to human resources and marketing. Our corporate teams are an essential part of our company, ensuring that we’re setting our businesses, clients, customers and employees up for success.

Human Resources plays a critical role in driving the employee experience, shaping the firm’s culture and building a diverse and inclusive workforce. We are a strategic partner to the business — working with leaders across the firm to hire, develop and retain the best people and help meet business goals. Together, we foster a work environment in which our people are supported, feel like they belong and are able to make an impact through their work. We develop and deliver a suite of products and services that help make JPMorgan Chase an employer of choice and drive our business forward.

title: Capacity or Resource Management-Advanced Associate
description:
At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence.

We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go.

Join EY and help to build a better working world.

JD – Rank 66 EMT Staff

Job Summary: We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented individual to join our Resource Management Team in an entry-level role. As a member of the team, you will play a crucial role in supporting resource planning and allocation across Tax. This position offers an excellent opportunity to gain practical experience in resource management while working alongside a dynamic and collaborative team.

Responsibilities:


Assist in resource planning activities, including analyzing project requirements, forecasting
resource needs, and identifying potential gaps or conflicts.
Collaborate with team members to gather resource availability information, update
databases, and maintain accurate records of resource allocations.


Support the scheduling and coordination of resources for projects, ensuring optimal utilization and alignment with project timelines.

Monitor and track resource allocation and utilization, providing regular updates and reports to leads and project managers.

allocation conflicts, working closely with

Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Management, or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a keen attention to detail.
Excellent organizational and time management abilities to handle multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
Effective communication skills, both verbal and written, to interact with team members and stakeholders at all levels.
Proficient in using productivity tools, such as Microsoft Excel, to analyze and present data.
Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment while also being able to work independently when required.
Proactive attitude and willingness to learn and adapt in a fast-paced and evolving work environment.

We offer a supportive work environment, opportunities for professional growth, and a competitive compensation package. If you are eager to kick-start your career in resource management and contribute to our organization's success, we invite you to apply for this entry-level position.

EY | Building a better working world

description:
Role: Software Engineer 1

Location: Bangalore (5 days WFO)

Experience: 0-2 Years (Entry Level)

Gender Diversity candidates only (Must)

Requirements

We are looking for a motivated and curious Software Engineer 1 to join our engineering team. This is a foundational role designed for a fresher who is eager to apply their academic knowledge to real-world problems, build scalable applications, and grow alongside a team of experienced developers.

Key Responsibilities

As a member of our engineering cohort, you will:


Develop & Maintain: Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and maintain high-quality software applications
Write Quality Code: Produce clean, efficient, and maintainable code while adhering to industry best practices and coding standards
Optimize Performance: Proactively troubleshoot and debug applications to ensure peak performance and a seamless user experience
Document: Assist in creating comprehensive technical documentation and user guides to support product scalability
Evolve: Engage in continuous learning, staying ahead of the curve with emerging technologies and modern development methodologies

Technical Stack: Proficiency in at least one major programming language: Go, Java, or Python

Mindset: An enthusiastic problem-solver who enjoys tackling complex challenges and thrives in a fast-paced environment
Education: BE/BTech degree, preferably from Tier 1 or Tier 2 institutions
Cloud Awareness: Familiarity with cloud ecosystems like AWS or GCP is a significant plus
Tooling: Hands-on experience with version control systems, specifically Git

title: HR Intern
description:
Instawork is on a mission to create meaningful economic opportunities for skilled hourly professionals in communities around the globe. Our AI-powered labor marketplace helps local businesses scale, and enables global technology companies to push the frontiers of robotics and AI. Backed by world-class investors like Benchmark, Spark Capital, Craft Ventures, Greylock, Y Combinator, and others, we’re looking for exceptional talent to reimagine the way the world works.

Location: Kormangala, Bangalore

Working Days: 6 Days a Week

Experience: Fresher (0–1 year)

Duration: 3 months

About the Role:

We are looking for a proactive and energetic HR Intern to support our day-to-day recruitment activities. This role is perfect for someone who wants to kick-start their career in HR, enjoys interacting with people, and thrives in a fast-paced startup environment.

Key Responsibilities:


Support end-to-end hiring for fresher and entry-level roles
Source, screen, and call candidates for various positions
Schedule and coordinate interviews with hiring panels
Maintain candidate trackers and ensure timely follow-ups
Assist in releasing offer letters and completing onboarding documentation
Ensure strong communication and coordination between candidates and internal stakeholders
Contribute to improving hiring processes and candidate experience



Requirements:


Good written and verbal communication skills
Strong organizational and follow-up abilities
Proactive attitude and willingness to learn
Comfortable working in a dynamic startup environment
Ability to manage multiple tasks at once
Available to work 6 days a week



Additional information:


Free Lunch + Snacks Daily
Cool workspace and fun team environment
Exclusive project goodies



Our Values


Empathy, Trust & Candor We put ourselves in the shoes of our colleagues and customers and don’t shy away from uncomfortable conversations, instead building trust through honest and direct feedback.
Bias for Action We practice high-velocity decision-making, clear-eyed that we often operate with incomplete information. Growing quickly means it’s OK to be wrong, so long as we learn from our mistakes and course correct!
Always Be Learning We’re a curious bunch, and with AI transforming our workplace we encourage everyone to learn from each other, compounding our knowledge and experience to help us change an entire industry.
Act Like an Owner We work long, hard, and smart, building products that delight our users and drive growth. Your ability to impact Instawork is limited only by your courage and conviction, not your job description.



About Instawork

Founded in 2015, Instawork is the nation’s leading online labor marketplace for food services, hospitality, light industry, and logistics, connecting more than 7M skilled workers with local restaurants, hotels, warehouses, stadiums, and more. Our AI-powered platform serves thousands of businesses across more than 50 major markets in the United States and Canada. We're not just helping fill shifts, we're supporting local economies—and we're just getting started!

Instawork has been featured by CBS News, The Wall Street Journal, The Washington Post, and the Associated Press. Forbes included us on their Next Billion Dollar Startups list; RetailTech Breakthrough named us Workforce Hiring Solution of the Year for 2025; and Inc. 5000 recognized us as one of the country's top 10% fastest-growing companies two years in a row. But what matters most is our impact. We're solving real problems for real people, and we’re doing it at scale.

Join our team to help us build something that matters! We’re looking for superstars who want to help us shape the future of work. With hubs in San Francisco, Bangalore, and Chicago, city offices in New York, Phoenix, and Singapore, we're back to working together in-person five days a week because we believe the best ideas happen when great people collaborate face-to-face. We also value diverse perspectives and encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds.

Ready to make an impact? Learn more at www.instawork.com/about.

Personnel Privacy Policy

title: Program Associate (Talent and Partnerships)
description:
Avistrat works with aerospace engineers and organisations to build design capability and deploy engineering capacity. We engage with industry across two dimensions of scale:




Vertical scale — by deepening technical capability, and execution layers within specific aerospace domains.
Horizontal scale — by expanding across teams, programmes, and partner organisations where credible engineering capacity is required.




On the capability side, we support organisations by augmenting their design engineering functions and strengthening technical depth through structured, project-oriented engagement.

On the capacity side, we work closely with industry partners to identify, prepare, and deploy engineering talent aligned to real-world aerospace requirements.




Our work sits at the intersection of:




Engineering design
Industry readiness
Talent deployment




We could be called an EdTech, SkillsTech or an engineering services company but are building Avistrat as a scalable, integrated aerospace company — one that grows in depth, breadth, and responsibility while maintaining high standards of quality, credibility, and long-term partnership.

This is a high-rigour, outcome-driven environment where judgment, ownership, and execution matter more than volume or optics.




ROLE OVERVIEW




This is an entry-level, execution-focused role providing hands-on exposure to how talent pipelines and client delivery are executed in the aerospace domain.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES




Candidate Intake & Decision Support:




- Own the first line of evaluation for candidates engaging with Avistrat

- Conduct structured conversations to assess readiness, seriousness, and fit

- Build judgment on who should move forward and who should not

- Prepare concise recommendations that directly inform leadership decisions




Talent Pipeline Ownership:




- Build and manage a live talent pipeline aligned to industry needs

- Shortlist candidates for internships, entry-level roles, and capacity-building mandates

- Coordinate execution from shortlisting to interview outcomes

- Track progress, identify bottlenecks, and close gaps proactively




Client & Partner Interface (Execution-Led):




- Support delivery of client capacity-building mandates

- Coordinate candidate–client interactions and timelines

- Gain exposure to real industry-facing execution with increasing responsibility




Process Building & Continuous Improvement:




- Convert live execution into repeatable processes and templates

- Analyse and improve tracking of candidates, conversations, and outcomes

- Contribute to making Avistrat’s talent pipeline scalable and predictable




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT




Not a sales role
Not a call-centre counselling role
Not an HR Admin role




WHAT WE LOOK FOR




A background and interest in the People or Human Resources function (academic or professional) is a must
Experience or demonstrated comfort with recruitment is a must
Some experience with aerospace would be ideal
Clear verbal and written communication
Comfort with ownership and follow-through
Ability to learn by doing and exploring fresh ideas
Ability to make and defend structured judgements
Attention to detail and professional conduct
Growth and improvement mindset
Drive to achieve goals




WHAT YOU WILL LEARN




How to thrive in a multi-stakeholder work environment
How to manage trade-offs for strategic decision-making
How quality of talent directly affects delivery outcomes
What enables successful client engagement
What the various elements of business operations are
How to grow and take ownership to drive work streams




GROWTH PATH




Freshers and early hires will join as interns for the first 3 months. Subsequently, the growth path could be expected as below:




0–6 months: Junior Talent and Partnerships Programme Associate (execution and judgment building)
6–12 months: Expanded ownership of intake, pipeline, and client delivery
12–18 months: Eligibility for progression to lead role, based on performance and business needs




There are various possible avenues along which career progression can be made based on candidate aspirations and business needs:




Ownership of the talent pipeline of Avistrat and organisational HR management
Programme management to manage a business function within Avistrat’s operating structure
Founder’s Office team to gain first-hand entrepreneurship insights




For further details about us, please visit our website: www.avistrat.com
